data.title: Bitcoin Up or Down - April 16, 12:40PM-12:45PM ET
data.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Bitcoin price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

slide1.title: 1. Pick a Polymarket
slide1.description: Buy ‘Yes’ or ‘No’ shares depending on your prediction. Odds shift in real time as other traders get in on the action.

slide2.title: 2. Place a Trade
slide2.description: Fund your account with crypto, debit card, or bank transfer—then you're ready to trade.

slide3.title: 3. Redeem

whatIsMarket.binaryA: "{title}" is a prediction market on Polymarket where traders buy and sell "Yes" or "No" shares based on whether they believe this event will happen. The current crowd-sourced probability is {leadingProbabilityDisplay} for "{leadingOutcome}." For example, if "Yes" is priced at {leadingProbabilityCents}, the market collectively assigns a {leadingProbabilityDisplay} chance that this event will occur. These odds shift continuously as traders react to new developments and information. Shares in the correct outcome are redeemable for $1 each upon market resolution.
whatIsMarket.multiWithSecondA: "{title}" is a prediction market on Polymarket with {outcomeCountDisplay} possible outcomes where traders buy and sell shares based on what they believe will happen. The current leading outcome is "{leadingOutcome}" at {leadingProbabilityDisplay}, followed by "{secondOutcome}" at {secondProbabilityDisplay}. Prices reflect real-time crowd-sourced probabilities. For example, a share priced at {leadingProbabilityCents} implies that the market collectively assigns a {leadingProbabilityDisplay} chance to that outcome. These odds shift continuously as traders react to new developments and information. Shares in the correct outcome are redeemable for $1 each upon market resolution.
whatIsMarket.multiWithoutSecondA: "{title}" is a prediction market on Polymarket with {outcomeCountDisplay} possible outcomes where traders buy and sell shares based on what they believe will happen. The current leading outcome is "{leadingOutcome}" at {leadingProbabilityDisplay}. Prices reflect real-time crowd-sourced probabilities. For example, a share priced at {leadingProbabilityCents} implies that the market collectively assigns a {leadingProbabilityDisplay} chance to that outcome. These odds shift continuously as traders react to new developments and information. Shares in the correct outcome are redeemable for $1 each upon market resolution.

activity.q: How much trading activity has "{title}" generated on Polymarket?
activity.highVolumeA: As of today, "{title}" has generated {totalVolumeRounded} in total trading volume{datePart}. This level of trading activity reflects strong engagement from the Polymarket community and helps ensure that the current odds are informed by a deep pool of market participants. You can track live price movements and trade on any outcome directly on this page.
activity.newMarketA: "{title}" is a newly created market on Polymarket{datePart}. As an early market, this is your opportunity to be among the first traders to set the odds and establish the market's initial price signals. You can also bookmark this page to track volume and trading activity as the market gains traction over time.

howToTrade.binaryA: To trade on "{title}," simply choose whether you believe the answer is "Yes" or "No." Each side has a current price that reflects the market's implied probability. Enter your amount and click "Trade." If you buy "Yes" shares and the outcome resolves as "Yes," each share pays out $1. If it resolves as "No," your "Yes" shares pay $0. You can also sell your shares at any time before resolution if you want to lock in a profit or cut a loss.
howToTrade.multiA: To trade on "{title}," browse the {outcomeCountDisplay} available outcomes listed on this page. Each outcome displays a current price representing the market's implied probability. To take a position, select the outcome you believe is most likely, choose "Yes" to trade in favor of it or "No" to trade against it, enter your amount, and click "Trade." If your chosen outcome is correct when the market resolves, your "Yes" shares pay out $1 each. If it's incorrect, they pay out $0. You can also sell your shares at any time before resolution if you want to lock in a profit or cut a loss.

currentOdds.q: What are the current odds for "{title}"?
currentOdds.noTradesA: This market was recently created and has not yet received its first trade. Be the first to set the opening odds by placing a trade, or bookmark this page to check back as the market develops.
currentOdds.binaryA: The current probability for "{title}" is {leadingProbabilityDisplay} for "{leadingOutcome}." This means the Polymarket crowd currently believes there is a {leadingProbabilityDisplay} chance that this event will occur. These odds update in real-time based on actual trades, providing a continuously updated signal of what the market expects to happen.
currentOdds.wideOpenWithSecondA: This is a wide-open market. The current leader for "{title}" is "{leadingOutcome}" at just {leadingProbabilityDisplay}, with "{secondOutcome}" close behind at {secondProbabilityDisplay}. With no outcome commanding a strong majority, traders see this as highly uncertain, which can present unique trading opportunities. These odds update in real-time, so bookmark this page to watch how the probabilities evolve.
currentOdds.wideOpenWithoutSecondA: This is a wide-open market. The current leader for "{title}" is "{leadingOutcome}" at just {leadingProbabilityDisplay}. With no outcome commanding a strong majority, traders see this as highly uncertain, which can present unique trading opportunities. These odds update in real-time, so bookmark this page to watch how the probabilities evolve.
currentOdds.standardWithSecondA: The current frontrunner for "{title}" is "{leadingOutcome}" at {leadingProbabilityDisplay}, meaning the market assigns a {leadingProbabilityDisplay} chance to that outcome. The next closest outcome is "{secondOutcome}" at {secondProbabilityDisplay}. These odds update in real-time as traders buy and sell shares, so they reflect the latest collective view of what's most likely to happen. Check back frequently or bookmark this page to follow how the odds shift as new information emerges.
currentOdds.standardWithoutSecondA: The current frontrunner for "{title}" is "{leadingOutcome}" at {leadingProbabilityDisplay}, meaning the market assigns a {leadingProbabilityDisplay} chance to that outcome. These odds update in real-time as traders buy and sell shares, so they reflect the latest collective view of what's most likely to happen. Check back frequently or bookmark this page to follow how the odds shift as new information emerges.

resolution.a: The resolution rules for "{title}" define exactly what needs to happen for each outcome to be declared a winner — including the official data sources used to determine the result. You can review the complete resolution criteria in the "Rules" section on this page above the comments. We recommend reading the rules carefully before trading, as they specify the precise conditions, edge cases, and sources that govern how this market is settled.

followWithout.q: Can I follow "{title}" without placing a trade?
followWithout.a: Yes. You don't need to trade to stay informed. This page serves as a live tracker for "{title}." The outcome probabilities update in real-time as new trades come in. You can bookmark this page and check the comments section to see what other traders are saying. You can also use the time-range filters on the chart to see how the odds have shifted over time. It's a free, real-time window into what the market expects to happen.

reliability.q: Why are Polymarket's odds for "{title}" considered reliable?
reliability.highVolumeSchemaA: Polymarket odds are set by real traders putting real money behind their beliefs, which tends to surface accurate predictions. With {totalVolumeRounded} traded on "{title}," these prices aggregate the collective knowledge and conviction of thousands of participants — often outperforming polls, expert forecasts, and traditional surveys. Prediction markets like Polymarket have a strong track record of accuracy, especially as events approach their resolution date. For example, Polymarket has a one month accuracy score of {accuracyScore}. For the latest stats on Polymarket's prediction accuracy, visit the accuracy page on Polymarket.

getStarted.q: How do I start trading on "{title}"?
getStarted.a: To place your first trade on "{title}," sign up for a free Polymarket account and fund it using crypto, a credit or debit card, or a bank transfer. Once your account is funded, return to this page, select the outcome you want to trade, enter your amount, and click "Trade." If you're new to prediction markets, click the "How it works" link at the top of any Polymarket page for a quick step-by-step walkthrough of how trading works.

priceMeaning.binaryQ: What does a price of {leadingProbabilityCents} for "{leadingOutcome}" mean?
priceMeaning.binaryA: On Polymarket, the price of "Yes" or "No" represents the market's implied probability. A "Yes" price of {leadingProbabilityCents} for "{title}" means traders collectively believe there is a {leadingProbability}% chance this event will happen. If you buy "Yes" at {leadingProbabilityCents} and the event does happen, you receive $1.00 per share — a profit of {payoutMathDisplay} per share. If the event doesn't happen, those shares are worth $0.
priceMeaning.multiQ: What does a price of {leadingProbabilityCents} for "{leadingOutcome}" mean?
priceMeaning.multiA: On Polymarket, the price of each outcome represents the market's implied probability. A price of {leadingProbabilityCents} for "{leadingOutcome}" in the "{title}" market means traders collectively believe there is roughly a {leadingProbabilityDisplay} chance that "{leadingOutcome}" will be the correct result. If you buy "Yes" shares at {leadingProbabilityCents} and the outcome is correct, you receive $1.00 per share — a profit of {payoutMathDisplay} per share. If incorrect, those shares are worth $0.

marketClose.resolvedA: The "{title}" market has been resolved. The final result has been determined and the market is no longer open for trading. You can still review the historical odds, outcome probabilities, and comments on this page to see how predictions evolved over time.
marketClose.endDatePassedA: The "{title}" market's scheduled end date has passed, but the market has not yet been officially resolved. The end date indicates when the underlying event is expected to occur or become knowable. It is not the moment trading stops. The market remains open for trading until the outcome is formally resolved through the resolution process. You can still buy, sell, or close your position while the market is pending resolution. Check the resolution status tracker and "Rules" section on this page for updates on the resolution timeline.
marketClose.noEndDateA: This market will resolve when the event's official outcome is determined. Check the "Rules" section on this page for the specific resolution criteria and timing.
marketClose.longTermA: The "{title}" market is a long-term prediction with a resolution date of {endDate} — that's roughly {timeUntilResolution} away. Long-term markets on Polymarket often see their odds shift significantly as events unfold, making them useful for tracking evolving sentiment. You can trade at any time or bookmark this page to follow the odds over the months and years ahead.
marketClose.standardA: The "{title}" market is scheduled to resolve on or around {endDate}. This means trading will remain open and the odds will continue to shift as new information emerges until that date. The exact resolution timing depends on when the official result becomes available, as outlined in the "Rules" section on this page.

tradersSaying.activeA: The "{title}" market has {communityPhrasing} where traders share their analysis, debate outcomes, and discuss breaking developments. Scroll down to the comments section below to read what other participants think. You can also filter by "Top Holders" to see what the market's biggest traders are positioned on, or check the "Activity" tab for a real-time feed of trades.
tradersSaying.newMarketA: The "{title}" market was recently created. Be one of the first to share your analysis by posting a comment below, or check back as the market grows to read what other traders think. You can also view the "Activity" tab for a real-time feed of recent trades.

whatIsPolymarket.a: Polymarket is the world's largest prediction market, where you can stay informed and profit from your knowledge of real-world events. Traders buy and sell shares on outcomes for topics ranging from politics and elections to crypto, finance, sports, tech, and culture, including markets like "{title}." Prices reflect real-time, crowd-sourced probabilities backed by financial conviction, often providing faster and more accurate signals than polls, pundits, or traditional surveys.

general.q1: What is esports trading on Polymarket?
general.a1: Polymarket lets you trade on the outcomes of professional esports matches across titles like CS2, League of Legends, Dota 2, Valorant, and more. Buy shares in the team you think will win — if they do, your shares pay out at $1 each.
general.a2: Polymarket covers major competitive titles including Counter-Strike 2, League of Legends, Dota 2, Valorant, Call of Duty, Overwatch, Mobile Legends, Rainbow Six Siege, Rocket League, Honor of Kings, StarCraft II, and more. New titles and tournaments are added regularly.
general.a3: Each match has a moneyline market where you can buy shares in either team. Prices reflect the community's estimated probability of each team winning. You can also trade on map handicaps, total maps, and individual map winners when available.
general.q4: Can I watch the matches while trading?
general.a4: Yes. When a live stream is available for a match, you'll see a "Watch Stream" button on the scoreboard. Clicking it opens a floating video player that you can drag, resize, and keep open while you continue browsing and trading.
general.a5: Markets are resolved based on official match results from tournament organizers. Once a match concludes, winning shares pay out at $1 and losing shares go to $0. Results are typically confirmed within minutes of a match ending.
general.a6: Polymarket is the world's largest prediction market platform. It lets you trade on the outcomes of real-world events — from politics and sports to crypto and culture — using real money. Prices reflect the crowd's real-time probability estimates for each outcome.

league.a1: Polymarket lets you trade on the outcomes of professional {league} matches. Buy shares in the team you think will win — if they do, your shares pay out at $1 each. You can trade on live and upcoming {league} matches from major tournaments worldwide.
league.a2: Each {league} match typically has a moneyline market where you pick the winner. Depending on the match format, you may also find map handicaps, total maps, and individual map or game winner markets.
league.q3: How do {league} odds work on Polymarket?
league.a3: Odds are expressed as share prices between 0¢ and 99¢. A team priced at 65¢ means the market gives them roughly a 65% chance of winning. If you buy at 65¢ and they win, you earn 35¢ profit per share. Prices update in real time as traders buy and sell.
league.q4: Can I watch {league} matches while trading?
league.a4: Yes. When a live stream is available, you'll see a "Watch Stream" button on the match scoreboard. It opens a floating video player you can drag and resize, so you can watch the action while browsing other markets or placing trades.
league.a5: Markets are resolved based on official results from tournament organizers. Once a match concludes, winning shares pay out at $1 and losing shares go to $0. Results are typically confirmed within minutes of a match ending.
league.a6: Polymarket is the world's largest prediction market platform. It lets you trade on the outcomes of real-world events — from politics and sports to crypto and culture — using real money. Prices reflect the crowd's real-time probability estimates for each outcome.

esportsTeam.q1: What {game} markets are available for {team} on Polymarket?
esportsTeam.a1: Polymarket currently hosts {marketCount} active markets for {team} ({teamAbbr}) with over ${volume} in total trading volume. You can trade on match winners, handicaps, totals, and various prop markets across all of their competitive fixtures. Each market is priced as an implied probability — for example, a price of 65¢ means traders collectively believe there is a 65% chance of that outcome. New markets are added as upcoming {team} matches are announced.
esportsTeam.a2: {team}'s active {game} roster currently has {rosterCount} players: {rosterNames}. Roster changes — including substitutions, stand-ins, and player transfers — can significantly affect market odds. This page updates automatically to reflect the latest official roster, so you can see exactly who is competing before trading on any {team} market.
esportsTeam.q3: What is {team}'s recent match record and win rate?
esportsTeam.a3: Based on their recent competitive matches, {team} has a {winRate}% win rate with a record of {matchRecord}. esportsTeam.q4: How do {game} prediction markets work on Polymarket?
esportsTeam.a4: Each {game} market on Polymarket is structured as a yes/no question — for example, "Will {team} win?" You buy shares in "Yes" or "No" at the current market price, which reflects the crowd-sourced implied probability. If your side is correct when the match concludes, your shares pay out $1 each. If incorrect, they pay $0. You can also sell your shares at any time before the match ends to lock in a profit or cut a loss. Prices update in real-time as traders react to news like roster changes, patch updates, and tournament developments.

whatIsMarket.a: The “{title}” market on Polymarket lets you trade on the outcome of the {leagueName} game between the {awayTeamFullName} and the {homeTeamFullName}, scheduled for {gameDate} at {gameTime} ET. The primary market is the moneyline — which team will win the game — where {favoredTeam} is currently priced at {favoredTeamPrice}¢ ({favoredTeamPrice}% implied probability) and {underdogTeam} at {underdogTeamPrice}¢ ({underdogTeamPrice}%). Beyond the moneyline, sports markets on Polymarket may feature spreads, totals (over/under), and player props, giving you multiple ways to trade on this game. Prices reflect real-time crowd-sourced probabilities. Shares in the correct outcome pay out $1 each when the market resolves after the game ends.

activity.q: How much trading activity has “{title}” generated on Polymarket?
activity.a: As of now, the “{title}” market has generated {totalVolume} in total trading volume across all market types (moneyline, spreads, totals, and player props). This volume reflects active engagement from the Polymarket trading community, and a deeper pool of traders generally means more informative, reliable odds. You can track live price movements and trade on any market directly on this page.

howToTrade.a: To trade on “{title},” start by choosing the market type you want to trade: Moneyline (which team wins), Spreads (margin of victory), Totals (combined score over/under), or Player Props (individual player stat lines). Each market shows the current price for each side — for example, the moneyline shows {awayTeamAbbreviation} at {awayTeamPrice}¢ and {homeTeamAbbreviation} at {homeTeamPrice}¢. Select the side you want to trade, choose Buy to take a position or Sell to close an existing one, enter your amount, and click Trade. If your chosen side is correct when the game ends and the market resolves, your shares pay out $1 each. If it’s incorrect, they pay out $0. You can also sell your shares at any time before the game ends to lock in a profit or cut a loss.

currentOdds.q: What are the current odds for “{title}”?
currentOdds.a: The current moneyline odds for “{title}” show {favoredTeamFullName} at {favoredTeamPrice}¢ ({favoredTeamPrice}% implied probability) and {underdogTeamFullName} at {underdogTeamPrice}¢ ({underdogTeamPrice}%). All odds update in real-time as traders buy and sell shares, reflecting the latest collective view of how this game will play out. Check back frequently or bookmark this page to follow how the odds shift as game time approaches.

resolution.a: The “{title}” market resolves based on the official final score of the {leagueName} game as reported by {leagueName}’s official results, including overtime if applicable. Moneyline markets resolve to the team that wins the game. Spread markets resolve based on the final margin of victory relative to the posted line. Totals (over/under) markets resolve based on the combined final score of both teams. Player prop markets resolve based on official box score statistics. If the game is postponed or canceled, the market resolution rules (available in the Rules section on this page) specify how that scenario is handled. We recommend reviewing the full resolution criteria before trading.

followWithout.q: Can I follow “{title}” without placing a trade?
followWithout.a: Yes. You don’t need to trade to stay informed. This page serves as a live odds tracker for the {awayTeamFullName} vs. {homeTeamFullName} game. The moneyline, spread, totals, and player prop odds all update in real-time as new trades come in. You can use the chart to track line movement as game time approaches — shifts in the odds often signal new information like injury updates, lineup changes, or sharp action from big traders. Bookmark this page, check the comments section to see what other traders are saying, and use the time-range filters on the chart to review how the odds have shifted. It’s a free, real-time window into what the market expects.

reliability.q: Why are Polymarket’s odds for “{title}” considered reliable?
reliability.schemaA: Polymarket odds are set by real traders putting real money behind their beliefs, which tends to produce well-calibrated probabilities. With {totalVolume} traded on “{title},” these prices aggregate the collective knowledge and conviction of active participants — similar to how traditional sportsbooks reflect sharp and public money, but in an open, transparent market anyone can participate in. Prediction markets have a strong track record of accuracy, especially as events approach their resolution. Polymarket’s overall one-month accuracy score is {accuracyScore}. For the latest stats, visit the accuracy page on Polymarket.

If you're new to prediction markets, click the “How it works” link at the top of any Polymarket page for a step-by-step walkthrough.

priceMeaning.q: What does a price of {teamAPrice}¢ for “{teamAAbbreviation}” mean?
priceMeaning.a: On Polymarket, the price of each side in a market represents the market's implied probability. A price of {teamAPrice}¢ for {teamAAbbreviation} on the series moneyline means traders collectively believe there is roughly a {teamAPrice}% chance that {teamA} will win this match. If you buy {teamAAbbreviation} shares at {teamAPrice}¢ and they win, you receive $1 per share — a profit of {teamAPayout}¢ per share. If they lose, those shares are worth $0. The same logic applies to individual {unit} winners, {unit} handicaps, and total {unitPlural}: a lower price means a higher potential payout but a lower implied probability of being correct.

marketClose.a: The “{title}” match is scheduled for {gameDate} at {gameTime} ET in the {tournamentName}. Trading remains open and odds will continue to shift as new information emerges leading up to the match. The market resolves once the series concludes and the official results are confirmed by the resolution source. The exact resolution timing depends on how many {unitPlural} are played in the Best of {boCount} series.
marketClose.aNoBoCount: The “{title}” match is scheduled for {gameDate} at {gameTime} ET in the {tournamentName}. Trading remains open and odds will continue to shift as new information emerges leading up to the match. The market resolves once the series concludes and the official results are confirmed by the resolution source.

tradersSaying.activeA: The “{title}” market has {commentCountDisplay} comments where traders share their analysis, debate match outcomes, and discuss breaking developments like roster changes and recent form. Scroll down to the comments section to join the conversation. You can also check the Top Holders tab to see how the market's biggest traders are positioned, or view the Activity tab for a real-time feed of trades being placed.
tradersSaying.newA: The “{title}” market's comments section is open for discussion — be the first to share your analysis on this match. Scroll down to the comments section to join the conversation. You can also check the Top Holders tab to see how the market's biggest traders are positioned, or view the Activity tab for a real-time feed of trades being placed.

whatIsPolymarket.a: Polymarket is the world's largest prediction market, where you can stay informed and trade on real-world events. Traders buy and sell shares on outcomes across politics, sports, crypto, finance, tech, culture, and esports — including tournaments and matches across League of Legends, CS2, Valorant, Dota 2, and more. Prices reflect real-time, crowd-sourced probabilities backed by real money, offering a transparent, market-driven alternative to traditional odds sources. Whether you're a seasoned esports trader or just getting started, Polymarket lets you put your knowledge to work.

whatIsMarket.a: "{title}" is a {windowLabel} prediction market on Polymarket where traders buy and sell shares on whether {cryptoName}'s price will finish higher ("Up") or lower ("Down") than its opening price over the {windowLabel} window specified in the title. The current market probability is {leadingProbabilityDisplay} for "{leadingOutcome}." A price of {leadingProbabilityDisplay} means the market collectively assigns a {leadingProbabilityDisplay} chance to that outcome. Prices update in real-time as traders react to live {cryptoName} price movements. Shares in the correct outcome are redeemable for $1 each upon market resolution.

activity.q: How much trading activity has "{title}" generated on Polymarket?
activity.highVolumeA: As of today, "{title}" has generated {totalVolumeRounded} in total trading volume. {cryptoName} Up or Down markets attract active traders reacting to live price movements in real time — this level of activity helps ensure the current Up/Down odds are informed by a deep pool of market participants. You can track live prices and place a trade directly on this page.
activity.lowVolumeA: "{title}" is an active short-term market on Polymarket. Trading volume can accumulate quickly as the {windowLabel} window progresses — jump in early to help set the odds before this window closes.

howToTrade.chainlinkA: To trade on "{title}," decide whether you believe {cryptoName}'s price will finish above or below the opening "Price to Beat"{priceToBeatPart}{endTimePart}. Buy "Up" if you think the price will rise, or "Down" if you think it will fall. Enter your amount and click "Trade." If your chosen outcome is correct at resolution, each share pays out $1.00. If incorrect, shares are worth $0. Because this market resolves in {windowDuration}, the window to exit your position before resolution is short — trade with that in mind.
howToTrade.hourlyA: To trade on "{title}," decide whether you believe {cryptoName}'s price will close higher ("Up") or lower ("Down") at the end of the {windowLabel} candle{startTimePart}. Buy "Up" if you think the close price will be higher than the open, or "Down" if you think it will be lower. Enter your amount and click "Trade." If your chosen outcome is correct at resolution, each share pays out $1.00. If incorrect, shares are worth $0.
howToTrade.dailyA: To trade on "{title}," decide whether you believe {cryptoName}'s price at noon ET on {resolutionDate} will be higher ("Up") or lower ("Down") than {cryptoName}'s price at noon ET on {referenceDate}. Buy "Up" if you think the price will rise day-over-day, or "Down" if you think it will fall. Enter your amount and click "Trade." If your chosen outcome is correct at resolution, each share pays out $1.00. If incorrect, shares are worth $0.

currentOdds.q: What are the current odds for "{title}"?
currentOdds.resolvedA: This {windowLabel} window has closed and resolved. The final outcome was "{outcome}." Use the time-range navigation bar at the top of this page to view adjacent windows or find the current live market.
currentOdds.liveA: The current probability for "{title}" is {leadingProbabilityDisplay} for "{leadingOutcome}," meaning the Polymarket crowd currently assigns a {leadingProbabilityDisplay} chance that {cryptoName}'s price will finish {upDown} over this {windowLabel} window. These odds update in real-time as traders react to live {cryptoName} price data. {urgency} Check back frequently or trade now before the window closes.

resolution.chainlinkA: The "{title}" market resolves based on whether {cryptoName}'s price at the end of the {windowLabel} window is greater than or equal to its price at the start of that window — if so, the outcome is "Up"; otherwise it is "Down." The resolution source is the Chainlink {cryptoTicker}/USD data stream. You can review the complete resolution criteria and data source in the "Rules" section on this page. We recommend reading the rules carefully before trading, as they specify the precise conditions, edge cases, and data sources that govern how this market is settled.
resolution.hourlyA: The "{title}" market resolves based on whether the closing price of the {cryptoName}/USDT 1-hour candle{startTimePart} on Binance is greater than or equal to its opening price — if so, the outcome is "Up"; otherwise it is "Down." The resolution source is Binance ({cryptoTicker}/USDT). You can review the complete resolution criteria and data source in the "Rules" section on this page.
resolution.dailyA: The "{title}" market resolves based on a comparison of {cryptoName}'s price at noon ET on {resolutionDate} versus noon ET on {referenceDate}, using Binance {cryptoTicker}/USDT 1-minute candle close prices. If the {resolutionDate} noon price is higher, the outcome is "Up"; if lower, "Down"; if equal, the market resolves 50-50. You can review the complete resolution criteria and data source in the "Rules" section on this page.

followWithout.q: Can I follow "{title}" without placing a trade?
followWithout.shortWindowA: Yes. You don't need to trade to stay informed. Each "{title}" page shows the live "Price to Beat"{priceToBeatPart} ({cryptoName}'s opening reference price for this window), the current live {cryptoName} price, and real-time Up/Down probabilities. Because each market window lasts {windowDuration}, individual pages resolve quickly — use the time-range navigation features at the top of the page to jump between windows and track how odds evolved across multiple intervals. You can also view the Order Book and Activity tab for a real-time feed of trade flow.
followWithout.hourlyA: Yes. You don't need to trade to stay informed. Each "{title}" page displays a live countdown timer showing how much time remains in the current hour, alongside real-time Up/Down probabilities. Use the time-range navigation features at the top of the page to browse past and upcoming hourly windows. You can also view the Order Book and Activity tab for a real-time feed of trade flow.
followWithout.dailyA: Yes. You don't need to trade to stay informed. Each "{title}" page shows real-time Up/Down probabilities reflecting trader sentiment on whether {cryptoName} will be higher or lower at noon ET tomorrow versus today. Use the date navigation features at the top of the page to browse past and upcoming daily windows. You can also view the Order Book and Activity tab for a real-time feed of trade flow.

reliability.q: Why are Polymarket's odds for "{title}" considered reliable?
reliability.highVolumeSchemaA: Polymarket odds are set by real traders putting real money behind their beliefs. With {totalVolumeRounded} traded on "{title}," the Up/Down probabilities reflect the collective judgment of traders watching {cryptoName}'s live price in real time. For {windowLabel} markets like this one, the odds represent an instantaneous crowd consensus on {cryptoName}'s price momentum — most meaningful as the window approaches its close, when traders have the most current price information available. For the latest stats on Polymarket's overall prediction accuracy, visit the accuracy page on Polymarket.
reliability.highVolumeBeforeLink: Polymarket odds are set by real traders putting real money behind their beliefs. With {totalVolumeRounded} traded on “{title},” the Up/Down probabilities reflect the collective judgment of traders watching {cryptoName}’s live price in real time. For {windowLabel} markets like this one, the odds represent an instantaneous crowd consensus on {cryptoName}’s price momentum — most meaningful as the window approaches its close, when traders have the most current price information available. "{title}" is still building its trading base for this window, so the current Up/Down probabilities may reflect only a small number of trades. As more participants enter before the window closes, the odds will become increasingly robust. For the latest stats on Polymarket's overall prediction accuracy, visit the accuracy page on Polymarket.

getStarted.q: How do I start trading on "{title}"?
getStarted.a: To place your first trade on "{title}," sign up for a free Polymarket account and fund it using crypto, a credit or debit card, or a bank transfer. Once your account is funded, return to this page — or navigate to the current active {windowLabel} window using the navigation bar at the top of the page — select "Buy Up" or "Buy Down," enter your amount, and click "Trade." If the window shown in the title has already closed, use the navigation bar to find the current live window. If you're new to prediction markets, click the "How it works" link at the top of any Polymarket page for a quick step-by-step walkthrough.

priceMeaning.q: What does a price of {leadingProbabilityCents} for "{leadingOutcome}" mean?
priceMeaning.a: On Polymarket, the price of each outcome represents the market's implied probability. A price of {leadingProbabilityCents} for "{leadingOutcome}" in the "{title}" market means traders collectively believe there is roughly a {leadingProbabilityDisplay} chance that {cryptoName}'s price will finish {upDown} over this {windowLabel} window. If you buy "{leadingOutcome}" shares at {leadingProbabilityCents} and {cryptoName} finishes in that direction, you receive $1.00 per share — a profit of {payoutMathDisplay} per share. If {cryptoName} moves the other way, those shares are worth $0. {volatilityNote}

marketClose.liveA: The "{title}" market is live and resolves{endTimePart}{timePart}. Trading is open. The Up/Down odds will continue shifting in real time as {cryptoName}'s price moves and traders react. Once the window closes, the market resolves based on {resolutionSummary}
marketClose.resolvedA: This {windowLabel} window closed{endTimePart} and has been resolved. The final outcome was "{outcome}." Use the navigation bar at the top of this page to view adjacent windows or find the current live market.
marketClose.pendingA: This {windowLabel} window closed{endTimePart} but has not yet been officially resolved. Resolution typically follows within seconds to minutes of the window closing. Check the Rules section on this page for the exact resolution process and timeline.

tradersSaying.activeA: The "{title}" market has {countDisplay} comments where traders share their analysis and react to live {cryptoName} price movements. Scroll down to the comments section to read what other participants think. You can also check the "Activity" tab for a real-time feed of trades on this window.
tradersSaying.newMarketA: No comments yet for this {windowLabel} window — check the "Activity" tab for a real-time feed of trades, or browse comments on other {cryptoName} Up or Down windows using the navigation bar above.

whatIsPolymarket.a: Polymarket is the world's largest prediction market, where you can stay informed and profit from your knowledge of real-world events. Traders buy and sell shares on outcomes for topics ranging from politics and elections to crypto, finance, sports, tech, and culture — including {windowLabel} {cryptoName} direction markets like "{title}." Prices reflect real-time, crowd-sourced probabilities backed by financial conviction, often providing faster and more accurate signals than polls, pundits, or traditional surveys.

windowUrgency.fiveminute: Because this window resolves in 5 minutes, odds can shift sharply in the final seconds.
windowUrgency.fifteen: Because this window resolves in 15 minutes, odds can move quickly as new price data comes in.
windowUrgency.hourly: As the hour progresses, odds reflect the latest live price action relative to the open.
windowUrgency.fourhour: Over a 4-hour window, odds shift as broader price trends develop.
windowUrgency.daily: Over a full day, odds reflect evolving sentiment as the day's price action unfolds.

priceVolatility.fiveminute: Because this market resolves in 5 minutes, prices can shift dramatically in a very short time.
priceVolatility.fifteen: Because this market resolves in 15 minutes, prices can move quickly as new price data comes in.
priceVolatility.hourly: Prices can shift meaningfully during the hour as {cryptoName}'s live price action evolves.
priceVolatility.fourhour: Over a 4-hour window, prices shift as broader trends and market sentiment develop.
priceVolatility.daily: Prices can shift throughout the day as {cryptoName}'s price action unfolds toward the noon ET close.

resolutionSummary.chainlink: whether the final Chainlink {cryptoTicker}/USD price is above or below the opening Price to Beat.
resolutionSummary.chainlinkWithPtb: whether the final Chainlink {cryptoTicker}/USD price is above or below the opening Price to Beat of {priceToBeat}.
resolutionSummary.hourly: whether the Binance {cryptoTicker}/USDT 1-hour candle closing price is above or below its opening price.
resolutionSummary.daily: whether {cryptoName}'s Binance price at noon ET on {resolutionDate} is higher or lower than at noon ET on {referenceDate}.
resolutionSummary.pyth: whether the Pyth-published price for {cryptoName} ({cryptoTicker}) at market close is higher or lower than the reference price at market open.
